stem: The number of stamps in a collection increased from 8 to 56. What was the percent increase in the number of stamps in this collection?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The percent increase equals (new - old)/old × 100%, so the percent increase is (56 - 8)/8 × 100% = (48/8) × 100% = 6 × 100% = 600%. Choice A is incorrect because 14% is approximately the new value as a fraction of the increase, not the percent increase. Choice B is incorrect because 48% is just the numerical difference 48 expressed as a percent rather than divided by the original. Choice D is incorrect; 700% would correspond to a final value of 64, not 56.
